Keep Flies Away with Lemons & Cloves 

Cut a lemon in half and press whole cloves into it before setting it on your picnic table or wherever you are eating outside. The strong scent of clove oil (especially eugenol) helps repel flies, while the citrus adds another fragrance they tend to avoid!🍋

Part 2: Click the link in my bio for all the recipes for the food I prepped and the make-ahead meals that helped feed our family vacation of 19. Part 1 was: Everything I made on vacation for our family of 19!   
Every year, our family rents one big vacation house and spends a week together. There are sandy feet, grandchildren climbing into bed for morning snuggles, late-night card games, and memories that somehow become family stories before the week is even over.  But feeding 19  people for a week doesn't happen by accident.
Over the years, I've learned that a little planning before we leave makes all the difference once we arrive. My goal isn't to spend vacation cooking.  It's to make sure everyone can relax, eat well, and enjoy themselves without constant grocery runs, takeout, or wondering what's for lunch.
Before we leave, I make everything I can ahead of time. Anything that travels well, reheats beautifully, or simply makes life easier gets checked off my list at home. Once we're there, every family takes a turn making dinner, which has become one of our favorite vacation traditions.

Click the link in my bio for the recipes for everything we made on our family vacation! With 19 people under one roof, someone is always hungry. There are early risers looking for breakfast, kids asking for snacks all afternoon, and somehow everyone is ready for dinner by six o’clock. 
The secret isn’t making fancy meals, it’s keeping things simple. Our favorite vacation meals are the ones that bring everyone together without keeping anyone stuck in the kitchen. We rely on fresh ingredients, family favorites, easy grilling recipes, make-ahead staples, and plenty of flexibility.
So excited to share everything we cooked, grilled, snacked on, and sipped during our vacation. If you’re feeding a crowd this summer or just looking for easy meal ideas that leave more time for making memories, I hope you’ll find plenty of inspiration.

Do those nasty ticks make you scared to go work in the yard or take a hike? Here is how I keep ticks away from me and my loved ones…..

Apple cider vinegar - Mix 1 part apple cider vinegar with 1 part water. Spray onto skin and clothing to help repel ticks.

Vicks VapoRub - The smell of menthol and camphor in this ointment may help repel ticks when applied to skin, clothing, shoes, and outdoor gear. I like to use a layer on my socks when I am working in the garden because socks are easily washed, and ankles and legs are prime low targets for ticks.

Lint Roller - Use a lint roller as soon as you come in from outside to check for ticks. Just roll the lint roller over skin and clothing, and any little buggers sticking to you will end up on the roller and not you! Kids find it fun to be rolled too, much better than regular old tick checks.

And that, my friends, is how you repel ticks and enjoy the great outdoors all summer long! XO, Babs.

#ticks #hacks #insectrepellent #Outdoors
The Basket House is all ready to celebrate and so are we!! Headed to lead the fife and drum corps in colonial garb. Happy 4th of July!! God Bless America 🇺🇸

After 40 years of family vacations, we have learned that the best trips aren't the ones that go perfectly but the ones where everyone pitches in, rolls with the unexpected, and makes room for a little fun along the way.

There will be piles of towels, dishes in the sink, and the occasional debate over sleeping arrangements. But years from now, no one will remember who took out the trash or who got the biggest bedroom. They'll remember the late-night card games, the belly laughs, the inside jokes, and the traditions that become family stories.

Family vacations aren't about perfection. They're about being together. And those memories are worth every bit of planning, flexibility, and teamwork it takes to make them happen. ❤️

My two golden rules for summer entertaining:

1) Always make a great dip. No gathering is complete without one. 

2) If you're entertaining outdoors, skip anything breakable. It makes the whole day more relaxed, and you never have to worry about accidents. 

My entire outdoor tablescape is from @Birch Lane, and I couldn't love it more. It's beautiful, practical, and made for easy summer entertaining.

Viral Pickle Dip

INGREDIENTS:
8 ounces cream cheese, softened
1 cup sour cream
1 tablespoon Grillo’s pickle juice
2 cups Grillo’s Pickles, finely chopped
1 cup sharp cheddar cheese, shredded
2 tablespoons fresh dill, chopped
1 teaspoon garlic powder
½ teaspoon onion powder
Freshly cracked black pepper, to taste
Potato chips, for serving

INSTRUCTIONS:
In a large bowl, combine the cream cheese, sour cream, and pickle juice until smooth and creamy.

Fold in the chopped pickles, cheddar cheese, dill, garlic powder, onion powder, and black pepper. 

Transfer to a shallow serving dish and spread into an even layer. Top with the remaining pickles, cheddar cheese, and fresh dill. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour before serving. Serve with ridged potato chips and veggies for dipping.

Some of the best summertime tricks have been passed down for generations. Here are a few old-fashioned favorites that are still worth knowing.

🍋 Keep Flies Away with Lemons & Cloves Cut a lemon in half and press whole cloves into it before setting it on your picnic table or wherever you are eating outside. The strong scent of clove oil (especially eugenol) helps repel flies, while the citrus adds another fragrance they tend to avoid.

🧺 Cover Food with a Damp Tea Towel Before plastic wrap, Grandma covered dishes with a clean, damp tea towel. The moisture weighs the towel down and helps create a cool barrier that keeps flying insects out while still keeping food moist.
  
🫖 Take a Tea Bath for Sunburn Steep 8-10 black tea bags, let the tea cool, and add it to a cool bath for a soothing soak. Black tea contains natural tannins and polyphenols that help calm irritated skin and take the “sting” out.  

🥄 The Hot Spoon Trick for Bug Bites Place a spoon in hot water for a few minutes, then gently press it against a mosquito bite for a few seconds. The warmth temporarily reduces the itch by affecting the proteins involved in your body's itch response.

🐝 Set Out a Can of Tuna for Yellow Jackets Place an open can of tuna 30–50 feet away from where everyone is eating. Yellow jackets are attracted to the protein and will head there instead of your picnic table, giving you a little more peace while you enjoy your meal.

🧱 Use Hot Bricks to Keep Food Warm Wrap bricks in foil, heat them on the grill, then place them in the bottom of a newspaper-lined cooler before adding your hot dishes. The bricks act as thermal mass, slowly releasing stored heat to help keep food warm for hours.

#QVCPartner Shopping has changed over the years, but one thing hasn’t. People still want honest recommendations from people they trust.

It was such a pleasure celebrating @QVC’s 40th Anniversary and seeing firsthand how they’re continuing to bring people together through live shopping, real conversations, and quality products.

Thank you, QVC, for including me in such a special day. Here’s to 40 years of Quality, Value, and Convenience, and to what’s next! ❤️ And make sure to keep shopping #QVCTikTokSuperBrandDay in their TikTok shop until 7/2. #LoveQVC
